public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as registrar.
manner: Treats loose talk like evidence waiting to harden into record and listens for contradiction before charm. In Shirsh, that reads as someone investigative, magically literate, and discreet, balancing inquiry with regulation, and the habits of a registrar still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of registrar in and around Magi Magistratus, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Magi Magistratus workable through the obligations of registrar while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Shirsh.
hidden_pressure: Must make memory legible under conditions that reward forgetting, misfiling, or selective recall. Around Magi Magistratus, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of registrar visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Orel Veilthorn can trade precise, actionable information once the party secures or clarifies the record they protect.
Orel Veilthorn needs help resolving a missing ledger, inconsistent testimony, or tampered record tied to Magi Magistratus.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Orel Veilthorn communicates in public.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Magi Magistratus: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague
